methods.name: glycopeptidepy.algorithm.PeptidoformGenerator.split_terminal_modifications
documentation:
Group modification rules into three classes, N-terminal,
C-terminal, and Internal modifications.

A modification rule can be assigned to multiple groups if it
is valid at multiple sites.

Parameters
----------
modifications : Iterable of ModificationRule
    The modification rules

Returns
-------
n_terminal: list
    list of N-terminal modification rules
c_terminal: list
    list of C-terminal modification rules
internal: list
    list of Internal modification rules

classes.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.residue.MemoizedResidueMetaclass
documentation:
A metaclass that memoizes Residues as they are constructed
by overriding the class __call__ method. It will attempt to
look up previously created residues by symbol, then by name.
If a previous instance is not found, it will be created and
saved.

Attributes
----------
_cache: dict

classes.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.residue.AminoAcidResidue

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.residue.AminoAcidResidue._by_name
documentation:
Configure this instance by name information

Parameters
----------
name : str
    Amino Acid Name

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.residue.AminoAcidResidue._by_symbol
documentation:
Configure this instance by symbol information,
by going from symbol to name, and from name to data

Parameters
----------
symbol : str
    Amino Acid symbol

Represent a single Amino Acid residue which compose peptide sequences. The
structure itself is intended to be immutable.

# Attributes

name: str

: The three letter abbreviation for the amino acid

symbol: str

: The single letter abbreviation for the amino acid

mass: float

: The neutral mass of the amino acid

composition: {class}`glypy.Composition`

: The chemical composition of the amino acid

chemical_class: str

: One of hydrophobic, polar_uncharged, positive_side_chain, negative_side_chain, or special_case,
  corresponding to the gross molecular properties of the amino acid

is_degenerate: bool:

: Whether this amino acid may stand for other amino acids

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.sequence.implementation.PeptideSequence.from_iterable
documentation:
Construct a {class}`PeptideSequence` instance from an arbitrary iterable of (amino acid, [modifications...])
pairs.

# Parameters

iterable: {class}`~.Iterable`

: An arbitrary {class}`~.Iterable` of two-element pairs where the first element denotes
  the amino acid at that position while the second is a list of modifications. These elements
  may be denoted by actual objects of the appropriate type or as strings (so long as all are are
  typed object or strings) and will be coerced accordingly.

glycan_composition: {class}`str` or {class}`~.GlycanComposition`

: An optional glycan composition given either as a string or a typed object. Will be coerced
  as needed.

n_term: {class}`~.TerminalGroup`

: The amino terminal group

c_term: {class}`~.TerminalGroup`

: The carboxyl terminal group

text: {class}`bool`

: Whether or not to enforce text parsing. Defaults to {const}`None` which results in automatic
  detection.

# Returns

{class}`PeptideSequence`

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.sequence.implementation.PeptideSequence.modified_residues
documentation:
Find all the modified residue positions in the sequence

# Returns

list of {class}`~.SequencePosition`

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.sequence.implementation.PeptideSequence.has_modification
documentation:
Check if a modification is present on the sequence

Parameters
----------
position: int
    The position in the sequence to check for the modification
modification_type: str or Modification
    The modification to check for.

Returns
-------
bool:
    Whether or not `modification_typ` is present at `position`

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.sequence.implementation.PeptideSequence.strip_modifications
documentation:
Return a copy of this sequence with all modifications removed.

# Returns

{class}`PeptideSequence`

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.sequence.implementation.PeptideSequence.get_fragments
documentation:
Generate fragments from this structure according to the strategy specified
by `strategy`, returning an iterator over the sequence of theoretical fragments.

There may be multiple fragments for a single position, resulting in the iterator
yielding lists of fragment objects per step.

# Parameters

kind

: The name of the ion series to produce fragments from, either as a string or the
  {class}`~.IonSeries` object to use

chemical_shifts

: A {class}`~.Mapping` between {class}`~.AminoAcidResidue` and a listof acceptable chemical shifts to apply
  to the produced fragments containing that {class}`~.AminoAcidResidue`, to be applied combinatorially.

strategy

: The strategy type to employ when producing fragments. Defaults to {class}`~.HCDFragmentationStrategy`.

include_neutral_losses: {class}`bool`

: Whether to include generic neutral losses (-NH3 and -H2O) on all fragments

kwargs

: Passed to `strategy`

# Returns

{class}`~.FragmentationStrategyBase`

: The fragmentation iterator

classes.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.sequence.implementation.NamedSequence
documentation: parse = Sequence

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.sequence.glycosylated_sequence.GlycosylatedSequenceMixin.glycan_fragments
documentation:
Generate all oxonium ions for the attached glycan, and
if `all_series` is `True`, then include the B/Y glycan
ion ladder, with the peptide attached to the Y ion ladder.

# Parameters

all_series: bool

: Generate the B/Y+peptide ion ladder, otherwise just 2-residue
  pairs for all monosaccharides in {attr}`self.glycan`

# Yields

SimpleFragment

modules.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.sequence.mutable_sequence
documentation: pylint: disable=assigning-non-slot

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.sequence.mutable_sequence.MutableSequenceMixin.drop_modification
documentation:
Drop a modification by name from a specific residue. If the
position is the N-term or the C-term, the terminal modification will
be reset to the default.

# Parameters

position: int

: The position of the modification to drop

modification_type: str or Modification

: The modification to drop

# Raises

ValueError:

: If the `modification_type` is not found at `position`, a {class}`ValueError` will
  be raised.

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.sequence.mutable_sequence.MutableSequenceMixin.add_modification
documentation:
Add a modification by name to a specific residue. If the
position is the N-term or the C-term, the terminal modification will
be replaced.

Parameters
----------
position: int
    The position of the modification to add
modification_type: str or Modification
    The modification to add

documentation:
Initialize a {class}`PeptideSequence` from a parse-able string.

sequence

: The string to parse

parser_function

: The parsing function to use

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.sequence.base._PeptideSequenceCore._init_from_components
documentation:
Initialize a {class}`PeptideSequence`'s state from a sequence of (
{class}`~.AminoAcidResidue`, \[{class}`~.Modification`, \]) pairs, with optional extra information
defining the glycan composition aggregate and the terminal groups

# Parameters

seq_list

: The sequence of ({class}`~.AminoAcidResidue`, \[{class}`~.Modification`, \])

glycan_composition

: The aggregate glycan composition associated with the sequence not fully specified
  by the modifications in `seq_list`.

n_term

: The N-terminal modification, if any

c_term

: The C-terminal modification, if any

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.sequence.base._PeptideSequenceCore._initialize_fields

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.sequence.base._PeptideSequenceCore.get_sequence
documentation:
Generate human readable sequence string. Called by {meth}`__str__`

include_glycan: bool

: Whether to include the glycan in the resulting string. Defaults to `True`

include_termini: bool

: Whether to include the N- and C-termini. Make sure this is `True` if you want non-standard
  termini to be properly propagated.

# Returns

str

Check if the definition line starts with `prefix`.

----------

    The prefix string to test for

bool

Check if the definition line ends with `suffix`.

bool

Hold parsed properties of a FASTA sequence's
definition line.

This object supports the {class}`Mapping` interface, and
keys may be accessed by attribute access notation.

# Attributes

defline

: The textual representation of definition line which
  lead to the collection of attributes stored therein.

classes.name: glycopeptidepy.io.fasta.UnparsableDeflineError
documentation: Indicate that a definition line could not be parsed by the current parser.

classes.name: glycopeptidepy.io.fasta.DefLineParserBase
documentation:
A base class for definition line parser, providing some common machinery in the {meth}`__call__` method.

This class requires that the subclass provide a {meth}`parse`
method to convert a string (without a ">" prefix). and return
an instance of {class}`.OrderedDict`.

classes.name: glycopeptidepy.io.fasta.CallableDefLineParser
documentation:
Parse a definition line using an arbitrary provided {class}`.Callable`.

# Attributes

callable_obj : Callable

classes.name: glycopeptidepy.io.fasta.RegexDefLineParser
documentation:
Parse a definition line using a provided
regular expression.

If {attr}`pattern` contains named groups, this will
produce a {class}`OrderedDict` where the group names
are keys, otherwise their index will be used as the
key.

# Attributes

pattern : re.pattern

classes.name: glycopeptidepy.io.fasta.DispatchingDefLineParser
documentation:
Attempt to parse a definition line using one of many
{class}`DeflineParserBase` instances.

# Attributes

graceful

: If {attr}`graceful`, failure to parse a defline
  with all of {attr}`parsers` results in a {class}`FastaHeader`
  no properties other than *defline*

header_type

: The type to use to contain the result of parsing.
  Defaults to {class}`FastaHeader`.

parsers

: A {class}`list` of {class}`DeflineParserBase` instances

classes.name: glycopeptidepy.io.fasta.FastaFileReader

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.io.fasta.FastaFileReader.reset
documentation:
Restart the iterator from the beginning of the sequence list.

Attempts to seek to the beginning of the file. If the input file
is not seekable, this will throw an exception.

A base class for parsing amino acid Fasta Files.

Supports sequential access through iteration, and random access if
{attr}`index` is {const}`True`.

classes.name: glycopeptidepy.io.fasta.ProteinFastaFileReader
documentation:
Parses Fasta Files into {class}`~.ProteinSequence` instances. Supports
sequential access, as well as random access when {attr}`index` is {const}`True`.

# Attributes

replace_unknown: {class}`bool`

: Whether to replace amino acids which are not recognized with an 'X'
  instead of throwing an error. If not set, the parser will replace
  the amino acids and emit a warning instead.

case_sensitive: {class}`bool`

: Whether to convert all characters in the amino acid sequence to upper case
  prior to parsing.

classes.name: glycopeptidepy.io.fasta.FastaIndexer

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.io.fasta.FastaIndexer.build_index
documentation:
Build an random access and metadata index over the stream
of Fasta File entries.

# Parameters

stream: file-like

# Returns

{class}`FastaIndex`

Encapsulates the process of building an index of byte-level offsets
and entry-level metadata for a Fasta file.

classes.name: glycopeptidepy.io.fasta.FastaIndex

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.io.fasta.FastaIndex.suffix
documentation:
Search for headers where `key` is a suffix of
the header.

This method builds a suffix tree which may be very
expensive in memory

# Parameters

key: {class}`str`

: The suffix to search for

# Returns

{class}`list`

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.io.fasta.FastaIndex.index_by
documentation: Build an extra index over the values of `key`

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.io.fasta.FastaIndex.clear_indices
documentation: Discard all the extra indices.

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.io.fasta.FastaIndex.query
documentation:
Query the index for headers whose keys match the values passed
through `kwargs`

# Parameters

queries: {class}`dict`, optional

: A mapping of {class}`FastaHeader` keys to values to match for
  inclusion in the result set. All pairs must match for a header
  to be included in the results.

kwargs:

: Additional key-value pairs to search for

# Results

{class}`list`:

: The {class}`FastaHeader` instances that matched all of the constraints
  the query.

A {class}`~.Mapping`-like object which stores the byte offsets
and entry metadata for a Fasta file.

functions.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.modification.target.get_position_modifier_rules_dict
documentation:
Labels the start and end indices of the sequence

A convenience dictionary initializer for ease-of-access in
{class}`ModificationTarget` site validation functions

# Parameters

sequence

: The sequence for which to project labels

protein_n_term

: Whether the sequence includes a protein N-terminal

protein_c_term

: Whether the sequence includes a protein C-terminal

# Return

defaultdict

classes.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.modification.target.ModificationTarget

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.modification.target.ModificationTarget.valid_site
documentation:
Return if a given residue at a sequence position (N-term, C-term, None)
is valid

Arguments
---------
amino_acid: AminoAcidResidue
    The amino acid to test
position_modifier: SequenceLocation
    The position in the sequence to test

Returns
-------
valid: bool
    Whether the match is valid
location_specification: SequenceLocation
    If the site is valid, this will be the type of location matched,
    otherwise it will default to SequenceLocation.anywhere

Specifies the range of targets that a given modification can be found at.

A single instance of {class}`ModificationTarget` describes one or more position/residue
rules for a single type of modification, bundled together underneath a single {class}`ModificationRule`
instance. {class}`ModificationTarget` may be extracted from Unimod data dictionaries using the
{meth}`from_unimod_specificity` class method.

# Attributes

amino_acid_targets: list

: The list of amino acid residues that this rule
  permits.

position_modifier: SequenceLocation

: The sequence-position specific filter
  this rule enforces.

Returns {const}`True` if the chemical shift results in a net loss in mass

Return the {class}`IonSeries` this fragment belongs to.

The {class}`IonSeries` this fragment is drawn from.

{class}`IonSeries`

Returns the chemical shift associated with the fragment.

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.fragment.FragmentBase.set_chemical_shift
documentation:
Sets the chemical shift associated with the fragment, updating
the {attr}`mass` and {attr}`chemical_shift`.

Base class for all Fragment types. Defines basic
name generation and neutral loss handling functions.

Attributes
----------
chemical_shift : ChemicalShift
    The ChemicalShift associated with this fragment, or None.
    If a ChemicalShift, its composition and mass are subtracted
    from this object's composition and mass attributes.
name: str
    The human readable description of this fragment
series: IonSeries
    The ion ladder this fragment is derived from

classes.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.fragment.PeptideFragment

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.fragment.PeptideFragment.base_name
documentation: Simply return string like b2, y3 with no modification information.

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.fragment.PeptideFragment.get_fragment_name
documentation: Build a complete fragment name listing any glycosylation modifications

Represents a peptide backbone fragment, such as a peptide b or y ion.

# Attributes

series: {class}`IonSeries`

: The ion series this fragment is from

bare_mass: float

: The unmodified mass of this fragment

mass: float

: The modified, correct mass of this fragment

position: int

: The peptide backbone position index this fragment came from, relative to the
  starting terminal

modification_dict: {class}`~.ModificationIndex`

: A count of the modifications on this fragment

flanking_amino_acids: list

: The adjacent amino acids of the amide bond that broke to form this fragment

glycosylation: dict

: A position to glycan mapping for glycan structures

chemical_shift: {class}`ChemicalShift`

: An associated chemical shift

composition: {class}`~.Composition`

: The elemental composition of this fragment

classes.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.fragment.IonSeries

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.fragment.IonSeries.get
documentation:
Get a particular cached {class}`IonSeries` by name

# Returns

{class}`IonSeries`

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.fragment.IonSeries.is_member
documentation:
Test if `key` matches this series name.

# Parameters

key: str

: The name of a fragment to test for membership

# Returns

bool

An ion series type, reflecting a terminal type and a bond cleavage type.

# Attributes

name: str

: The name of the ion series, like 'b' or 'y'

direction: int

: Whether the fragments from this ion series arise from the N-terminal (1),
  C-terminal (-1), or neither (0).

includes_peptide: bool

: Whether the fragment includes the peptide backbone or not.

mass_shift: float

: The mass delta applied to the raw components of the fragment.

composition_shift: {class}`~.Composition`

: The elemental composition delta applied to the raw components of the fragment.

functions.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.modification.data.unimod._formula_parser
documentation:
Parse a unimod formula composed of elements,
isotopes, and other bricks.

In order to look up a Brick's composition, this
function must have access to a session.

Parameters
----------
formula: str
    A Unimod formula of the form `A(n) B(m)...`
    where A, B, ... are element names or bricks and
    (n), (m)... are parenthesized possibly signed integers or
    omitted in which case they are interpreted as 1
session: Session
    An active SQLAlchemy session for looking up bricks in the database

Returns
-------
CompositionType

functions.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.modification.data.unimod._composition_listener
documentation:
Attach event listeners to an InstrumentedAttribute
to trigger formula parsing on load and on change.

functions.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.modification.data.unimod.has_composition
documentation:
A decorator to simplify flagging a Model with a column
to be treated as a formula for parsing. Calls {func}`_composition_listener`
internally.

functions.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.modification.data.unimod.create

Parse the relational table-like XML file provided by http://www.unimod.org/downloads.html
and convert each <tag>_row into an equivalent database entry.

By default the table will be held in memory.

{class}`~.Modification`

An abstract collection of {class}`~.ModificationRule` objects.

This class primarily deals with loading rules from file sources and
managing global states. It should not be used instantiated directly,
instead, use {class}`ModificationTable`.

classes.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.modification.source.ModificationTableBase
documentation:
A mixin class to provide implementation for {class}`ModificationSource`
methods for {class}`ModificationTable`

classes.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.modification.source.ModificationTable

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.modification.source.ModificationTable.register_alias
documentation:
Add a new name to an existing rule, and make that name
resolvable.

# Parameters

name

: The name of an existing {class}`~.ModificationRule`

alias

: The new name to add to the rule denoted by `name`.

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.modification.source.ModificationTable.rules
documentation:
The set of all {class}`~.ModificationRule` instances in this object

# Returns

set

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.modification.source.ModificationTable.add
documentation:
Add a new {class}`~.ModificationRule` to this object.

If `rule` shares names with other rules and their masses match,
they will be merged.

# Parameters

rule

: The rule to add. If is a {class}`Mapping`, it will be coerced
  into a {class}`~.ModificationRule`

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.modification.source.ModificationTable.remove
documentation:
Remove an existing {class}`~.ModificationRule` from this object.

If `rule` is a {class}`~.ModificationRule`, all of its `names` will be
removed. If `rule` is a {class}`str`, then only that name will be removed.

# Parameters

rule

: Either a {class}`~.ModificationRule`, or a single {class}`str` denoting
  the name of the rule to remove.

A {class}`~.Mapping` connecting modification names to {class}`~.ModificationRule`
instances.

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.modification.source.RestrictedModificationTable.collect_available_modifications
documentation:
Clears the rules stored in the core dictionary (on self, but not its data members),
and copies all information in the data member sub-tables into the core dictionary

A few base types for doing type-based behavior changes

A base type for classes describing peptide sequence modifications

classes.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.base.ResidueBase

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.modification.modification.ModificationInstanceBase.is_a
documentation:
Returns whether or not this {class}`ModificationRule` object belongs to
the specified {class}`~.ModificationCategory`.

# Returns

bool

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.modification.modification.ModificationInstanceBase.is_tracked_for
documentation:
Determine if {attr}`rule` is tracked by a particular
behavioral pattern associated with a {class}`~.ModificationCategory`.

This relationship is distinct from {meth}`is_a` which merely observes that
the semantic relationship holds, not that any actual behavior is available.

# Parameters

category

: The category to check

# Returns

bool

classes.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.modification.modification.Modification

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.modification.modification.Modification._resolve_name
documentation:
Look up a {class}`~.ModificationRule` by name in {attr}`modification_table`.

If the modification rule has not been registered with {attr}`modification_table`, and `rule_string`
is not a full modification specification string, this may fail.

# Parameters

rule_string

: The name or specification of a {class}`~.ModificationRule`

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.modification.modification.Modification.modification_table
documentation:
Access the class-wide {class}`~.ModificationTable` instance which all dynamic name
lookups pass through.

# Returns

{class}`~.ModificationTable`

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.modification.modification.Modification.from_rule
documentation:
Create a new {class}`Modification` instance from a {class}`~.ModificationRule`
instance directly.

This method is marginally faster than the default initialization behavior which
checks to see if the rule is a string.

# Parameters

rule: {class}`~.ModificationRule`

: The rule this modification will follow

# Returns

{class}`Modification`

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.modification.modification.Modification.__init__
documentation:
Instantiate a {class}`Modification` instance from a template.

# Parameters

rule

: Either the name of a modification rule as a string (to be resolved with
  {meth}`_resolve_name`) or an actual {class}`~.ModificationRule`, which will
  be used to populate all other attributes.

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.modification.modification.Modification.names
documentation:
Returns the alternative names for this {class}`Modification`'s {attr}`rule`

# Returns

frozenset

Represents a peptide modification, with a fixed mass and optionally
a composition.

{class}`Modification` objects are usually bound to a {class}`~.ModificationRule`
object which defines their properties. Though the essential properties are copied
into the {class}`Modification` instance, the rule still holds additional metadata
and behaviors.

Modifications are equal to anything that their {attr}`rule` is equal to by name,
and hash based upon their {attr}`name`.

# Attributes

name: str

: The preferred name of the modification

mass: float

: The monosisotopic mass of the modification

composition: {class}`~.Composition`

: The elemental composition of the modification. This
  attribute may be missing when the modification is not
  defined from a molecule but an arbitrary mass shift.

rule: {class}`~.ModificationRule`

: The rule that defines the modification type this object represents
  an instance of.

modules.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.fragmentation_strategy.peptide
documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

modules.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.modification.rule
documentation:
Represent rules describing peptide modifications with
particular chemical properties, specificities, and other
metadata.

The core data structure is {class}`ModificationRule`, which
in addition to its chemical composition, mass, target specificities,
and categorical descriptors, carries a substantial amount of information
about what its names are.

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.modification.rule._ModificationResolver.register
documentation:
Register a {class}`~.ModificationRule` subclass with
the parsing cascade this object represents.

This method may be used as a decorator for target classes.

# Parameters

rule_type: {class}`ModificationRule` subclass

: The subclass to register

# Returns

{class}`ModificationRule` subclass

: The `rule_type` value, allowing this method to be used
  as a decorator.

classes.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.modification.rule.ModificationRuleBase

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.modification.rule.ModificationRuleBase.is_a
documentation:
Returns whether or not this {class}`ModificationRule` object belongs to
the specified {class}`~.ModificationCategory`.

# Returns

bool

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.modification.rule.ModificationRuleBase.is_tracked_for
documentation:
Determine if this {class}`ModificationRule` is tracked by a particular
behavioral pattern associated with a {class}`~.ModificationCategory`.

This relationship is distinct from {meth}`is_a` which merely observes that
the semantic relationship holds, not that any actual behavior is available.

# Parameters

category

: The category to check

# Returns

bool

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.modification.rule.ModificationRuleBase.is_standard
documentation:
Indicates whether the {class}`ModificationRule` describes a modification which
has been specified from a reference source, and may be fully reconstructed from
its name alone.

This property is not strictly enforced, and does not cover all aspects of the object's
state. It does however provide a general guide for whether or not a modification is able
to be translated from {mod}`glycopeptidepy`'s internal representation into something that
another program *should* be able to understand.

# Returns

bool

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.modification.rule.ModificationRuleBase.serialize
documentation: A string representation for inclusion in sequences
Represent common properties of modification rules.

classes.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.modification.rule.ModificationRule

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.modification.rule.ModificationRule.reduce
documentation:
Given a collection of {class}`ModificationRule` instances, combine
those with the same name into a single object using {meth}`ModificationRule.__add__`

# Parameters

rules

: The rules to collapse

# Returns

list

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.modification.rule.ModificationRule.from_protein_prospector
documentation:
Build a {class}`ModificationRule` from data scraped from Protein Prospector

# Parameters

amino_acid_specificity

: The amino acid and position target specificity

modification_name

: The name of the modification

title

: The display title for the modification, by default None

monoisotopic_mass

: The monoisotopic mass shift of the modification, by default None

neutral_losses

: Any neutral losses associated with the modification, by default None

# Returns

{class}`ModificationRule`

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.modification.rule.ModificationRule.from_unimod
documentation:
Createa a {class}`ModificationRule` instances from a {class}`dict` scraped
from Unimod.

# Parameters

unimod_entry

: The Unimod entry description for this modificatoin

# Returns

{class}`ModificationRule`

Represent the name, mass, and position specifities associated with a given modification.
Additionally stores information on metadata about this rule computed from these values and
categorical information from expert sources.

# Attributes

categories

: The list of named categories of PTMs that this modification
  falls under. Examples are "artefact", "glycosylation", or "biological process"

mass

: The mass of the modification defined by this rule

name

: A common name for the modification defined by this rule

names

: The set of all names used to refer to this modification, beyond those
  commonly used and those for display purposes.

aliases

: The set of all names that are allowed to be used to display this modification

options

: Arbitrary information from external sources about this modification
  rule and its phenomena.

title

: A formal or "full" name for this modification that may be its chemical name
  or some other more verbose representation which is used to identify it but
  not convenient to read or write.

targets

: All applicable {class}`.ModificationTarget` instances which apply to this
  rule. This set may differ from instance to instance of the same rule by
  configuration as per the {class}`RestrictedModificationTable`, for example

classes.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.modification.rule.AnonymousModificationRule
documentation:
Construct a modification rule from a string that can be placed
in a peptide sequence and be reconstructed without being stored
in a {class}`ModificationTable` instance and backed by a modification
database.

"...(@\{name}-\{mass})..."

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.modification.rule.AminoAcidSubstitution._simple_sequence_mass
documentation:
Because AminoAcidSubstitution may contain sequence-like entries that
need massing but we cannot import `sequence` here, this defines a simple
sequence list to total mass without any terminal groups, but handles residues
and modifications.

For a full sequence_to_mass converter, see the sequence module

modules.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.glycan
documentation: pylint: disable=unsubscriptable-object

classes.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.glycan.GlycanCompositionProxy
documentation:
A mapping-like object that imitates the GlycanComposition interface in
a read-only fashion.

classes.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.glycan.GlycanCompositionWithOffsetProxy
documentation:
A {class}`GlycanCompositionProxy` that pretends to allow you to
mutate the {attr}`composition_offset` attribute of the underlying
composition, but instead stores that within the proxy, leaving the
underlying compostion unchanged.

This type should be useful for re-using the same {class}`FrozenGlycanComposition`
object without repeatedly copying and invalidating its cached properties. When
this object is copied, the underlying GlycanComposition is not copied, only
the proxy.

modules.name: glycopeptidepy.utils.memoize
documentation: Implementation of memoizing decorators

classes.name: glycopeptidepy.utils.memoize.MemoizedFunction

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.utils.memoize.MemoizedFunction.memoize
documentation:
Make a memoization decorator. A negative value of `maxsize` means
no size limit.

A memoized function wrapper that manages its own hash table cache.

The wrapped function's arguments must be hashable.

Attributes
----------
max_size: int
    The maximum number of distinct invocations to cache
function: Callable
    The wrapped function
cache: Mapping
    The hash table cache
lru: bool
    Whether or not to enforce a LRU eviction strategy

classes.name: glycopeptidepy.utils.memoize.FragmentCachingMixin
documentation:
Implements a cache over get_fragments() that saves the last arguments and result
value, and returns it instead of recomputing result if the next invocation's arguments
match.

functions.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.parser.sequence_tokenizer
documentation:
A simple stateful sequence parser implementing a formally context-free language
describing components of a polypeptide sequence with N-, C- and internal modifications,
as well as a glycan composition written at the end.

# Return

chunks: list
mods: list
n_term: str
c_term: str
glycan: {class}`glypy.composition.glycan_composition.FrozenGlycanComposition`

functions.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.parser.prefix_to_postfix_modifications
documentation:
Transform a chunk-list from modification-on-the-left to
modification-on-the-right association rules.

This code does not work with terminal modifications

Return
------
list

functions.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.parser.sequence_length
documentation:
Compute the peptide length of a sequence string.
This parses the sequence and counts the number of chunks.

Return
------
int

functions.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.parser.strip_modifications
documentation:
A wrapper around `sequence_tokenizer` that discards all modifications

Return
------
str

functions.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.parser.sequence_tokenizer_respect_sequons
documentation:
A wrapper around `sequence_tokenizer` that will treat an n-glycan sequon as a single unit

Return
------
list

functions.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.parser.parse_simple
documentation:
Parse a simple sequence string without *any* modifications
into a chunked token list, matching the return signature of {func}`sequence_tokenizer`

functions.name: glycopeptidepy.enzyme.cleave
documentation:
A reimplementation of pyteomics.parser.cleave which produces leaky cleavages
of a peptide sequence by a regex rule. Includes the cut indices, not included in
pyteomics.

classes.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.fragmentation_strategy.glycan.CADFragmentationStrategy
documentation:
Generate glycopeptide fragments derived from glycosidic
bond cleavages as in lower energy CAD fragmentation

classes.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.fragmentation_strategy.glycan.StubGlycopeptideStrategy

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.structure.fragmentation_strategy.glycan.StubGlycopeptideStrategy.n_glycan_composition_fragments
documentation:
Generate theoretical N-glycan Y fragment compositions containing the core motif
plus a portion of the extended branches if {attr}`extended` is used. Unless :attr:extend

# Parameters

glycan

: Description

core_count

: The total number of glycans attached to the peptide

iteration_count

: The core index. Unless {attr}`extended_fucosylation` is true, this will
  limit the number of Fucose residues to one per core at most, and if
  the iteration count exceeds the number of cores no Fucose will be added

# Returns

list of dict

: The compositions corresponding to the theoretical fragments

A fragmentation strategy that generates intact peptide + glycan Y fragments from
glycopeptides where the glycan structure is not fully known. When the structure is
known, {class}`CADFragmentationStrategy` should be used instead

# Attributes

extended

: Whether to fragment beyond the conserved core

extended_fucosylation

: Whether to consider multiple `Fuc` residues per fragment rather than one per core

classes.name: glycopeptidepy.utils.sequence_tree.PrefixTree

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.utils.sequence_tree.PrefixTree.add
documentation:
Adds a single Sequence-like object to the tree
structure, placing the ith member of `sequence` on
the ith level of `self`. At each level, if `label`
is not None, it is added to the level's `labels` set.

Parameters
----------
sequence : Sequence
    Sequence-like object to add to the tree
label : object, optional
    An arbitrary object which denotes or is associated with
    `sequence`

Returns
-------
self

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.utils.sequence_tree.PrefixTree.add_ngram
documentation:
Adds successive prefixes of `sequence`
to the tree by calling {meth}`add`. This process
is identical to calling {meth}`add` once for a prefix
tree.

# Parameters

sequence

: Sequence-like object to add to the tree

label

: An arbitrary object which denotes or is associated with
  `sequence`

A hash-based Prefix Tree for testing for
sequence inclusion. This implementation works for any
slice-able sequence of hashable objects, not just strings.

classes.name: glycopeptidepy.utils.sequence_tree.SuffixTree
documentation:
A hash-based Suffix Tree for testing for
sequence inclusion. This implementation works for any
slice-able sequence of hashable objects, not just strings.

functions.name: glycopeptidepy.utils.intervals.iterative_build_interval_tree

Builds an IntervalTreeNode hierarchy from `intervals`. This

intervals : Iterable of Intervals

    the Interval Tree

Returns
-------
IntervalTreeNode
    The root of the constructed Interval Tree

classes.name: glycopeptidepy.utils.intervals.Interval
documentation:
A generic wrapper around data associated
with a particular interval over a single dimension

Attributes
----------
data : dict
    A holder of arbitrary extra data not associated
    with any single member contained in this interval
end : Number
    The end point of the interval described
members : list
    A list of arbitrary objects which are associated with this
    interval.
start : Number
    The start point of the interval described

classes.name: glycopeptidepy.utils.intervals.IntervalTreeNode

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.utils.intervals.IntervalTreeNode.contains_point
documentation:
Returns the list of contained intervals for all
nodes which contain the point `x`.

Parameters
----------
x : Number
    The query point

Returns
-------
list
    A list of objects which span `x` from all spanning
    nodes' `contained` list.

methods.name: glycopeptidepy.utils.intervals.IntervalTreeNode.overlaps
documentation:
Returns the list of all contained intervals which
overlap with the interval described by `start` and `end`

Parameters
----------
start : Number
    The start of the query interval
end : Number
    The end of the query interval

Returns
-------
list
    A list of all objects which overlap the argument interval
    from all spanning nodes' `contained` list.

A single node in an Interval Tree. The
root node of an interval tree can be treated
as the tree itself.

Attributes
----------
center : Number
    The center point of this node's collection
contained : list
    The list of Interval-like objects which
    were aggregated under this node. This collection
    of intervals all span this node's center.
end : Number
    The end point of this node's collection or it's
    rightmost child's end point, whichever is larger
left : IntervalTreeNode
    The left child node of this node. May be `None`
    if this node is a leaf node. Contains all
    intervals whose end point is less than this
    node's center
level : int
    Depth in the tree
parent : IntervalTree
    This node's parent node. May be None if this
    node is the root node.
right : IntervalTree
    The right child node of this node. May be `None`
    if this node is a leaf node. Contains all
    intervals whose start point is greater than this
    node's center
start : Number
    The start point of this node's collection or it's
    leftmost child's start point, whichever is smaller
